answer:
Your last cholesterol reading from April 5th, 2026 shows:

Total Cholesterol: 237 mg/dL (higher than ideal)
LDL: 162 mg/dL (the "bad" cholesterol — elevated)
HDL: 38 mg/dL (the "good" cholesterol — low)
Triglycerides: 186 mg/dL (elevated)

These results suggest your cholesterol levels need attention. You're already on blood pressure medications (Telmisartan and Amlodipine), and your doctor referred you to a cardiologist around this time — that's the right step.
Your next conversation with your doctor should cover whether you need cholesterol-lowering medication and lifestyle changes like diet and exercise.
Not medical advice — talk to your doctor before acting on this.
